Items.Title: 在 React 中动态操作子项 | Manipulating Children dynamically in React
Items.Description:
学习如何在 React 中为子组件动态添加功能可以增强您构建灵活、可重用和可定制组件的能力。 它提倡良好的软件设计🖥️原则，例如组合、可重用性和可扩展性，使您的代码更具可维护性和适应不断变化的需求。
喝杯咖啡☕，准备好和我一起学习一个最重要的概念🤓

我们都需要知道，当以只读模式使用 React unique prop 时，您的组件将完全按照给定的方式使用它。 然而，如果我告诉你，你可以更进一步，以任何方式、形状或形式改变你孩子的组成部分，你会怎样？ 让我们了解如何使用它们来动态操作渲染输出。

强大的 React API 是 -

句法

React.cloneElement(element,[props],[...children])

它是 React 顶级 API 的一部分，用于操作和转换元素。 您可以将 react 作为全局对象和文件的顶部导入，并将它们作为该对象的方法进行访问。 或者作为一个命名的 import 。

 //As global object React.cloneElement(element,[props],[...children])

或者

import {cloneElement} from "react" //To use it cloneElement(element,[props],[...children])

第一个参数是你想要克隆的反应元素，

  • 第二个参数是将添加并与传递到组件的原始道具合并的提示。
  • 第三个参数是克隆对象的子对象。 请注意，不会复制现有对象的子项

    • 请记住，React 中的提示是不可变的对象。 您必须首先创建元素的副本并在副本中执行转换。这正是 React.cloneElement 允许您实现的。

    例如，您可以向下面所示的提交按钮元素动态添加另一个道具。

     const buttonElement={ type:SubmitButton, prop:{ color:"green", children:"submit" } }; const output=React.cloneElement(buttonElement,{disable:false}) //Adding another prop dynamically to submit button element { type: SubmitButton, props:{ color:"green", children:"submit", disable:false }, }

    修改子属性

    • 重复字符
    • 花式子按钮
    • 一次性修改单选按钮属性
    • 克隆另一个 React 元素作为 prop
  • 添加到子属性
    • 加粗字体
    • 将 prop 传递给通过 React.cloneElement() 接收的元素
  • 扩展子组件的功能

    • 另一个对子操作有用的重要顶级 API 是 react.children，它提供了处理 props.children 数据结构的实用程序。
    注意：- props.children 是保存传递给组件的嵌套内容的 prop，而 React.Children 提供实用方法来处理和操作子项。

    最重要的方法是地图功能。 其他方法是

    • 让我们以这个 API 的方法之一为例：

      传递给 map 函数的第一个参数应该是 children 属性，第二个参数是在 children 属性中包含的每个子项中调用的函数。 回想一下，children prop 是所有 React 组件都具有的特殊 prop，它提供了一种特殊的数据结构，类似于数组，您可以在其中执行迭代。 然而，它们并不完全是 JavaScript 数组的实例。 这就是为什么要遍历所有兄弟姐妹，你应该使用 React 提供的特殊React.children.map API。

      在地图投影函数中，您应该首先使用React.cloneElement克隆元素，将目标子元素作为第一个参数传递，将所有新道具的配置作为第二个参数传递。 生成的元素将具有原始元素的道具，并合并了新的道具。

      由于RadioGroup收到一个selected属性，它是一个指向已选择选项值的字符串，因此在任何时间点，checked 仅对其中一个选项为真。 这是通过执行相等性检查，将RadioOption value prop 与selected值进行比较来保证的。

      最后， RadioGroup组件通过将新的RadioOptions元素括在花括号中来返回它们。

      您必须正确连接 props 值、checked 和 onChange。 首先， value 和 checked 道具都应该按原样传递给无线电输入。 然后，您应该使用单选输入中的 onChange 事件，从事件目标对象中检索 value 属性并将其作为参数传递给 onChange 属性，如下所示。 这样就完成了 RadioOption 组件的实现。

       export const RadioOption = ({ value, checked, onChange, children }) => { return ( <div className="RadioOption"> <input id={value} type="radio" name={value} value={value} checked={checked} onChange={(e) => { onChange(e.target.value); }} /> <label htmlFor={value}>{children}</label> </div> ); };

      用于演示上述代码的 Codesandbox链接。

      输出 -

      通过使用这种模式，父组件可以将额外的数据或功能传递给子组件，从而允许动态行为。 下面是一个示例来说明如何使用 render prop 模式动态添加功能：

       // Parent component function ParentComponent() { return ( <div> {/* Render prop being used */} <ChildComponent render={(data) => <p>{data}</p>} /> </div> ); } // Child component function ChildComponent({ render }) { const data = "Dynamic functionality added!"; // Calling the render prop function passed from the parent return render(data); }

      从 render prop 函数返回的内容然后由子组件渲染。

      希望你能很好地掌握所有的概念👐 如果你一直读到这里😇，别忘了点个赞💗 发表评论让我知道你对这个博客的看法。

      如果感到震惊，请随时与我联系并在评论部分发表您的疑问，我会尽力回答他们。

      关注我了解更多此类基本驱动概念👍

      如果 srishtikprasad 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

      笔记：

      感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 srishtikprasad：

      降低 srishtikprasad 的所有帖子的可见度

    原文:

    Learning how to add functionality dynamically to children in React enhances your ability to build flexible, reusable, and customizable components. It promotes good software design 🖥️principles such as composition, reusability, and extensibility, making your code more maintainable and adaptable to changing requirements.
    Grab a cup of coffee ☕ and get ready to learn one of the most important concept with me 🤓

    We all need to be aware that when utilising a react unique prop in read-only mode, your components will take it exactly as it is given. What if I told you, however, that you could go much further and alter the components of your children in any way, shape, or form? Let's learn how they can be used to manipulate the Render Output dynamically.

    The powerful react APIs are -

    React.cloneElement,React.children, and Render props

    1. React.cloneElement()

    Syntax

    React.cloneElement(element,[props],[...children])

    It is part of the React top-level API and it's used to manipulate and transform elements. You can either import react as a global object and the top of your file and access them as methods on that object. Or alternatively as a named import.

    //As global object
React.cloneElement(element,[props],[...children])

    OR

    import {cloneElement} from "react"
//To use it
cloneElement(element,[props],[...children])

    React.cloneElement effectively clones and returns a new copy of a provided element.

    • The first argument is the react element you would like to clone,
    • the second argument is the prompts that will be added and merged with the original props passed into the component.
    • the third argument is the children of the cloned object. Note that the children of the existing object are not copied

    Remember that prompts in react are immutable objects. You must create a copy of the element first and perform the transformation in the copy.That's exactly what React.cloneElement allows you to achieve.

    For example you could add another prop dynamically to the submit button element illustrated below.

    const buttonElement={ type:SubmitButton, prop:{ color:"green", children:"submit" }
}; const output=React.cloneElement(buttonElement,{disable:false}) //Adding another prop dynamically to submit button element  { type: SubmitButton, props:{ color:"green", children:"submit", disable:false },
}

    React.cloneElement() is used within the definition of a parent component to perform the following processes:

    1. Modify children properties
      • Repeated characters
      • Fancy child button
      • Modify radio button attributes at a go
      • Clone another React element as a prop
    2. Add to children properties
      • Bold text
      • Pass prop to an element received via React.cloneElement()
    3. Extend the functionality of children components

    2. React.Children

    Another important top-level API useful for children manipulation is react.children, which provides utilities for dealing with the props.children data structure.
    NOTE:- props.children is the prop that holds the nested content passed to a component, while React.Children provides utility methods to work with and manipulate the children.

    The most important method is the map function. React.children.map is very similar to the map function from arrays and invokes a function in every child contained within its children prop, performing a transformation and returning a new element.
    Other methods are

    • React.Children.toArray
    • React.Children.count
    • React.Children.only

    let's take example of one of the method of this API:

    The API for the RadioGroup component is defined as two props: selected, which is a string that matches one of the RadioOption values and onChange, which is the event that gets called whenever a selection changes, providing the new value as an argument.

    Code of App.js

    import { RadioGroup, RadioOption } from "./Radio";
import { useState } from "react";
export default function App() { const [selected, setSelected] = useState(""); return ( <div className="App"> <h1>How did you hear about our company?</h1> <RadioGroup onChange={setSelected} selected={selected}> <RadioOption value="Social media">Social media</RadioOption> <RadioOption value="friends">friends </RadioOption> <RadioOption value="Advertisement">Advertisement</RadioOption> <RadioOption value="others">others </RadioOption> </RadioGroup> </div> );
}

    The RadioOptions variable should be assigned to the return value of React.Children.map, which will be a new React element. The first argument passed to the map function should be the children prop, and the second is a function that gets invoked in every child contained within the children property. Recall that a children prop is a special prop all React components have and that it presents a special data structure, similar to arrays, where you can perform iterations. However, they are not exactly instances of JavaScript arrays. That’s why to iterate over all siblings you should use the special React.children.map API provided by React.

    Inside the map projection function, you should first clone the element using React.cloneElement, passing as first argument the target child element and as a second argument a configuration with all new props. The resulting element will have the original element’s props with the new props merged in.

    onChange can be passed to each child (RadioOption) as it is and checked is the property the RadioOption uses to determine if the underlying radio input is selected. Since RadioGroup receives a selected property, which is a string pointing to the value of the option that has been selected, checked will be only true for one of the options at any point in time. This is guaranteed by performing an equality check, comparing the RadioOption value prop with the selected value.

    Finally, the RadioGroup component returns the new RadioOptions elements by wrapping them in curly braces.

    Code of Radio.js

    export const RadioGroup = ({ onChange, selected, children }) => { const RadioOption = React.Children.map(children, (child) => { return React.cloneElement(child, { onChange, checked: child.props.value === selected }); }); return <div className="RadioGroup">{RadioOption}</div>;
}; export const RadioOption = ({ value, checked, onChange, children }) => { return ( <div className="RadioOption "> <input id={value} type="radio" name={value} value={value} checked={checked} onChange={(e) => { onChange(e.target.value); }} /> <label htmlFor={value}>{children}</label> </div> );
};

    The value prop is already provided explicitly inside the App.js component and children represents the label text for the radio input.

    You have to connect the props value, checked and onChange correctly. First, both value and checked props should be passed to the radio input as is. Then, you should use the onChange event from the radio input, retrieve the value property from the event target object and pass it to the onChange prop as the argument as seen below. That completes the implementation of the RadioOption component.

    export const RadioOption = ({ value, checked, onChange, children }) => { return ( <div className="RadioOption"> <input id={value} type="radio" name={value} value={value} checked={checked} onChange={(e) => { onChange(e.target.value); }} /> <label htmlFor={value}>{children}</label> </div> ); };

    Codesandbox Link for demonstrating above code.

    Output -

    3. Render props

    The render prop pattern in React is often used to dynamically add functionality to child components. The render prop pattern is a technique where a component receives a function as a prop, typically named "render" or "children," which it then calls to render its content. By using this pattern, the parent component can pass additional data or functionality to the child component, allowing for dynamic behaviour. Here's an example to illustrate how the render prop pattern can be used to add functionality dynamically:

    // Parent component
function ParentComponent() { return ( <div> {/* Render prop being used */} <ChildComponent render={(data) => <p>{data}</p>} /> </div> );
} // Child component
function ChildComponent({ render }) { const data = "Dynamic functionality added!"; // Calling the render prop function passed from the parent return render(data);
}

    The ParentComponent renders the ChildComponent and passes a function as the render prop. The ChildComponent calls this function and passes the data as an argument. The returned content from the render prop function is then rendered by the child component.

    Hope you get all the concepts very well👐 If you are reading till here 😇, do not forget to hit like💗 Do comment and let me know how did you like the blog.

Items.Title: 我正在为 React 开发一个新的服务器状态管理库。 | I'm developing a new server state management library for React.
Items.Description:
我很高兴向社区介绍我开发的 RSM（Redux 状态管理器）工具。 在这篇文章中，我想与大家分享创建RSM背后的动机。

在我的公司，我们使用带有异步 thunk 的 Redux 来管理服务器状态。 虽然 Redux 通过集中式状态管理带来了许多好处，但它也有一些缺点。 例如，将所有 reducer 组合到一个存储中会导致初始 JavaScript 文件过大。 此外，即使使用 Redux Toolkit，我们仍然需要编写大量重复代码。 于是，公司里的高级工程师一直在考虑更换Redux，但是至今没有找到合适的包。

为什么不使用 SWR 或 React Query？

实际上，我们已经尝试将 SWR 和 React Query 合并到我们内部控制台类型的网站中，同事们发现 React Query 比 SWR 更人性化。 虽然 React Query 在控制台类产品中表现不错，但大多数同事认为它不太适合我们的主要产品网站。

我们的产品是一个用户论坛，每天都有大量的用户访问。 这里有一个同事认为React Query不适合我们产品的例子：当用户创建一条新评论时，我们希望对应的帖子的totalCommentCount增加一。 从 React Query 的角度来看，我们应该在创建评论后执行以下代码：

 queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts', 'get', postId] });

这样，React Query 会自动在后台请求新的帖子，并更新对应的帖子。 然而，考虑到我们的帖子响应非常大，获取整个帖子只是为了更新totalCommentCount似乎很浪费。 您可能认为我们可以这样做：

 queryClient.setQueryData(['posts', 'get', postId], oldPost => { const totalCommentCount = oldPost.totalCommentCount + 1; return { ...oldPost, totalCommentCount }; });

但是这种方法有一个问题。 当用户返回帖子列表时，列表中的 totalCommentCount 不会更新，因为 queryKey 不同。 对于细心的用户来说，这可能看起来很奇怪。 当然，我们可以像这样添加更多的代码：

 queryClient.setQueryData(['posts', 'list'], oldPosts => { const oldPost = oldPosts.find(post => post.id === postId); if (!post) return oldPosts; const totalCommentCount = oldPost.totalCommentCount + 1; const newPost = { ...oldPost, totalCommentCount }; const oldPostIndex = oldPosts.indexOf(oldPost); const newPosts = [...oldPosts]; newPosts.splice(oldPostIndex, 1, newPost); return newPosts; });

这样，我们就考虑到了更新列表的场景。 但这真的那么简单吗？ 我们的列表可以有多种形式，例如“流行”、“最新”以及具有自己列表的不同论坛。 如果我们把这些场景都考虑进去，可能会给我们带来比使用 Redux 还要大的精神负担，更何况有些 API 响应是这样的格式：

 { "items": [], "nextKey": "123" }

如果我们必须使用上述方法对数据进行变异，那将是一场灾难。 而且， 它违背了 React Query 推荐我们使用的实用方式。 虽然 React Query 非常适合控制台类型的网站，但不幸的是，它似乎不太适合我们的主网站。

那么，是什么让 React Query 不适合我们的主站呢？ 我相信这是对数据的控制级别。 使用 Redux 时，更新帖子会自动更新列表中的相应帖子。 但是，在使用 Redux 时，它不像使用useQuery检索数据那样简单。 我们需要编写大量的 action 和 reducer，如果我们要添加去重和重新验证等功能，编写的代码量会增加更多。 显然，Redux 不是最佳选择。

既然我们还没有为我们的用例找到合适的包，为什么不自己开发呢？ 这就提出了可维护性的问题。 如果我们创建了一个只有我们自己使用的工具，那么维护它的责任就完全落在我们身上。 缺乏社区支持是高级同事非常关心的问题。

作为一名初级开发人员，我一直对状态管理问题很感兴趣。 因此，我想尝试开发自己的满足公司需求的工具作为我的副业。 当然，我也希望这个工具可以帮助其他正在为管理服务器状态而苦苦挣扎的开发人员。

要实现的目标

首先，让用户能够完全控制他们的数据至关重要。 与为我们处理服务器状态管理的 React Query 不同，所有数据写入都是用户定义的。 虽然这可能需要用户编写更多的代码，但我认为这是一种必要的权衡，而且相对于 Redux，编写的代码量相对较少。

另一个关键点是提供一个简洁且用户友好的钩子，类似于useQuery ，它允许开发人员从任何组件调用它而不必担心重复请求。 此外，轮询和重新验证等功能也很重要。

如果您对这个项目有任何想法或建议，请随时与我分享。 谢谢。

原文:

I'm excited to introduce the RSM (Redux State Manager) tool I've developed to the community. In this article, I would like to share with everyone the motivation behind creating RSM.

In my company, we use Redux with async thunk to manage server state. While Redux brings many benefits with centralized state management, it also comes with some drawbacks. For example, combining all reducers into a single store leads to excessively large initial JavaScript files. Additionally, even with Redux Toolkit, we still need to write a lot of repetitive code. As a result, senior engineers in the company have been considering replacing Redux, but so far, we haven't found a suitable package.

Why not use SWR or React Query?

Actually, we have tried incorporating both SWR and React Query into our internal console-type websites, and colleagues find React Query to be more user-friendly than SWR. Although React Query performs well in console-type products, most colleagues believe it is not quite suitable for our main product website.

Our product is a user forum that receives a large number of user visits every day. Here's an example that colleagues think React Query is not suitable for our product: when a user creates a new comment, we want the corresponding post's totalCommentCount to increase by one. From the perspective of React Query, we should execute the following code after creating a comment:

queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['posts', 'get', postId] });

This way, React Query will automatically request the new post in the background and update the corresponding post. However, considering that our post response is quite large, fetching the entire post just for updating totalCommentCount seems wasteful. You might think we can do it this way instead:

queryClient.setQueryData(['posts', 'get', postId], oldPost => { const totalCommentCount = oldPost.totalCommentCount + 1; return { ...oldPost, totalCommentCount };
});

But there's a problem with this approach. When the user goes back to the post list, the totalCommentCount on the list won't update because the queryKey is different. This may appear odd to observant users. Of course, we can add more code like this:

queryClient.setQueryData(['posts', 'list'], oldPosts => { const oldPost = oldPosts.find(post => post.id === postId); if (!post) return oldPosts; const totalCommentCount = oldPost.totalCommentCount + 1; const newPost = { ...oldPost, totalCommentCount }; const oldPostIndex = oldPosts.indexOf(oldPost); const newPosts = [...oldPosts]; newPosts.splice(oldPostIndex, 1, newPost); return newPosts;
});

This way, we take into account the scenario of updating the list. But is it really that simple? Our list can have various forms, such as "popular," "latest," and different forums with their own lists. The queryKey might look like this:

const allPopular = ['posts', 'list', 'popular', 'all'];
const allLatest = [('posts', 'list', 'latest', 'all')];
const forumPopular = [('posts', 'list', 'popular', forumId)];
const forumLatest = [('posts', 'list', 'latest', forumId)];

If we also consider all these scenarios, it might bring us even more mental burden than using Redux, not to mention some API responses have this format:

{ "items": [], "nextKey": "123"
}

If we have to mutate the data using the methods mentioned above, it would be a disaster. Moreover, it goes against the practical way React Query recommends us to use. While React Query fits well with console-type websites, unfortunately, it seems less suitable for our main website.

So, what makes React Query unsuitable for our main website? I believe it's the level of control over the data. React Query focuses on managing server state for us, which means we don't have as much control over the data compared to using Redux. When using Redux, updating a post would automatically update the corresponding post in the list. However, when using Redux, it's not as straightforward as using useQuery to retrieve the data. We need to write a lot of actions and reducers, and if we want to add features like deduplication and revalidation, the amount of code to write increases even more. Clearly, Redux is not the optimal choice.

Since we haven't found a suitable package for our use case, why not develop our own? This brings up the issue of maintainability. If we create a tool that only we use, then the responsibility of maintaining it falls solely on us. Lack of community support is a significant concern for senior colleagues.

As a junior developer, I have always been interested in state management problems. Therefore, I want to try developing my own tool that meets the company's needs as my side project. Of course, I also hope this tool can help other developers who are struggling with managing server state.

Goals to achieve

First and foremost, it is essential to empower users to have full control over their data. Unlike React Query, where server state management is handled for us, all data writes will be user-defined. Although this may require users to write more code, I believe it is a necessary trade-off, and compared to Redux, the amount of code to write is relatively less.

Another crucial point is to provide a concise and user-friendly hook, similar to useQuery, that allows developers to call it from any component without worrying about duplicate requests. Additionally, features like polling and revalidation are also important.

If you have any ideas or suggestions regarding this project, please feel free to share them with me. Thank you.

Here is the repo of RSM: github.com/jason89521/react-server-model

Items.Title: AZ：Git 备忘单 | A-Z: Git Cheat Sheet
Items.Description:
布拉克博杜罗格鲁

发表于 6 月 10 日

  • 它由 Linux 操作系统的创造者 Linus Torvalds 在 2005 年创建。
  • 它允许您查看更改历史、恢复到以前的版本以及管理不同的开发分支。

Repository:

  • 存储库，通常称为“repo”，是 Git 存储所有文件及其完整历史记录的中心位置。 它包含项目的所有版本和有关更改的元数据。

Commit:

  • 提交是项目在特定时间点的快照。 它表示对文件所做的一组更改。 每个提交都有一个唯一的标识符，它包括作者、时间戳和提交消息等信息。

Branch:

  • 分支是存储库中的一条单独的开发线。 它允许多个开发人员同时处理不同的功能或错误修复，而不会干扰彼此的代码。

Merge:

  • 合并是将更改从一个分支集成到另一个分支的过程。 它合并提交并解决更改重叠时出现的任何冲突。 这通常用于将功能分支中所做的更改带回主分支（通常称为“master”或“main”）。

Remote:

  • 远程是托管在服务器上的 Git 存储库的副本，允许开发人员之间进行协作。 流行的远程存储库托管服务包括 GitHub、GitLab 和 Bitbucket。

Pull and Push:

  • 拉取是指从远程存储库中检索最新的更改并将它们合并到本地存储库中。 推送意味着将您的本地更改发送到远程存储库，使它们可供其他协作者使用。

有用的 Git 命令：

  • 在当前目录中初始化一个新的 Git 存储库。 它创建一个新的 .git 子目录来存储版本控制信息。
  • 在本地计算机上创建远程存储库的副本。 它下载所有文件及其整个历史记录。
  • 向暂存区添加文件或更改。 这准备要提交的文件。
 git commit -m "<message>"
  • 将暂存区域的快照记录为存储库中的新提交。 提交消息描述了提交中所做的更改。
  • 显示存储库的当前状态。 它显示哪些文件被修改、暂存或未跟踪。
  • 显示当前更改与上次提交之间的差异。 它显示了修改文件中的逐行差异。
  • 列出存储库中的所有分支。 当前分支标有星号。
  • 切换到不同的分支。 它更新工作目录以匹配指定的分支。
  • 将指定分支合并到当前分支。 它集成了更改并创建了一个新的提交。
  • 从远程存储库中获取最新的更改并将它们合并到当前分支中。 它通常用于使用其他人所做的更改来更新本地存储库。
  • 将本地提交推送到远程存储库。 它使用您的更改更新远程存储库，使它们可供其他人使用。
  • 显示存储库中的提交历史记录。 它显示提交消息、作者、时间戳和提交标识符。
 git remote add <name> <url>
  • 将远程存储库与名称和 URL 相关联。 它允许您通过给定名称引用远程存储库。
  • 列出与当前存储库关联的所有远程存储库及其 URL。
  • 临时保存尚未准备好提交的更改。 它允许您在不提交或放弃更改的情况下切换分支。
  • 检索最近隐藏的更改并将它们应用于当前分支。 一旦应用更改，它就会从存储中删除更改。
  • 它通过创建包含相反更改的新提交来撤消先前的提交。 它允许您安全地撤消更改而无需重写历史记录。

原文:

Burak Boduroğlu

Posted on Jun 10

 
  • Git is a distributed version control system widely used in software development.
  • It was created by Linus Torvalds, the creator of the Linux operating system, in 2005.
  • Git allows multiple developers to collaborate on a project by keeping track of changes made to files and coordinating the merging of those changes.

Version Control:

  • Git is primarily used for version control, which means it keeps track of changes made to files over time. It allows you to view the history of changes, revert to previous versions, and manage different branches of development.

Repository:

  • A repository, often referred to as a "repo," is a central location where Git stores all the files and their complete history. It contains all the versions of the project and metadata about the changes.

Commit:

  • A commit is a snapshot of the project at a specific point in time. It represents a set of changes made to the files. Each commit has a unique identifier, and it includes information such as the author, timestamp, and a commit message.

Branch:

  • A branch is a separate line of development within a repository. It allows multiple developers to work on different features or bug fixes simultaneously without interfering with each other's code.

Merge:

  • Merging is the process of integrating changes from one branch into another. It combines the commits and resolves any conflicts that arise when changes overlap. This is often used to bring the changes made in a feature branch back into the master branch (usually called "master" or "main").

Remote:

  • A remote is a copy of a Git repository that is hosted on a server, allowing collaboration between developers. Popular remote repository hosting services include GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket.

Pull and Push:

  • Pulling refers to retrieving the latest changes from a remote repository and merging them into the local repository. Pushing means sending your local changes to the remote repository, making them available to other collaborators.

Useful Git Commands:

  • Initializes a new Git repository in the current directory. It creates a new .git subdirectory that stores the version control information.
  • Creates a copy of a remote repository on your local machine. It downloads all the files and their entire history.
  • Adds a file or changes to the staging area. This prepares the file to be committed. 
git commit -m "<message>"
  • Records a snapshot of the staging area as a new commit in the repository. The commit message describes the changes made in the commit.
  • Shows the current status of the repository. It displays which files are modified, staged, or untracked.
  • Displays the differences between the current changes and the last commit. It shows the line-by-line differences in modified files.
  • Lists all the branches in the repository. The current branch is marked with an asterisk.
  • Switches to a different branch. It updates the working directory to match the specified branch.
  • Merges the specified branch into the current branch. It integrates the changes and creates a new commit.
  • Fetches the latest changes from a remote repository and merges them into the current branch. It is often used to update the local repository with changes made by others.
  • Pushes local commits to a remote repository. It updates the remote repository with your changes, making them available to others.
  • Shows a history of commits in the repository. It displays commit messages, authors, timestamps, and commit identifiers. 
git remote add <name> <url>
  • Associates a remote repository with a name and URL. It allows you to refer to the remote repository by the given name.
  • Lists all the remote repositories associated with the current repository along with their URLs.
  • Temporarily saves changes that are not ready to be committed. It allows you to switch branches without committing or discarding the changes.
  • Retrieves the most recently stashed changes and applies them to the current branch. It removes the changes from the stash once they have been applied.
  • It undoes a previous commit by creating a new commit that contains the opposite changes. It allows you to safely undo changes without rewriting history.

蒂斯里·桑德拉特拉娜

发表于 6 月 10 日

在新的 Chromebook (ChromeOS) 或任何 Linux 发行版上设置开发环境可能是一项耗时且艰巨的任务。 但不要害怕！ 介绍Crosup ，这是一个功能强大的命令行工具，旨在简化和加快流程。 借助Crosup ，您可以快速配置您的开发环境、安装必要的工具并立即开始编码。 在本文中，我们将探讨Crosup的功能、安装过程、用法和配置选项，使您能够简化开发工作流程。

使 Crosup 脱颖而出的功能

  1. 兼容性：Crosup 兼容各种操作系统，包括 ChromeOS、基于 Debian 的 Linux 发行版、OpenSUSE、Fedora、RedHat、CentOS、Alpine、Arch 和 Gentoo。 无论您喜欢哪种平台，它都能确保无缝体验。

  2. 易于安装：只需一个命令，您就可以在您的机器上快速安装 Crosup。 只需运行bash <(curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tsirysndr/crosup/master/install.sh)让魔法展开。

  3. 丰富的开发人员工具：Crosup 提供了大量可供安装的开发人员工具。 从 Docker、Nix 和 Homebrew 到 VS Code、fzf 和 kubectl，Crosup 都能满足您的需求。

  4. 避免维护自定义 Bash 脚本： Crosup消除了维护自定义 bash 脚本以设置开发环境的需要。 相反，它提供了一个标准化且易于配置的解决方案，可以节省您的时间和精力。

安装变得简单

要开始使用Crosup ，您需要执行一个简单的安装过程。 只需按照前面提到的命令， Crosup就会在您的系统上启动并运行。

使用变得简单

通过执行crosup install命令，您可以根据需要轻松安装各种工具。 此外， Crosup还提供help和init等子命令，以提供进一步的帮助并生成默认配置文件。

通过配置简化您的工作流程：
默认情况下， Crosup包含嵌入式配置，但您可以使用 crosup init 命令生成默认配置文件。 以 HCL/TOML 格式编写的配置文件允许您指定工具、包、版本检查、安装前和安装后命令，甚至依赖项。

基于 Debian 的系统的示例配置：

我们将探索一个最小的Crosfile.toml配置，它安装 Vim 和 Git：

 # Crosfile.toml [install.pkg.vim] packages = ["vim"] version_check = "vim --version" [install.pkg.git] packages = ["git"] version_check = "git --version"

贡献和许可

如果您有兴趣改进 Crosup，请查看该项目的贡献指南。 结论

它的兼容性、简单性和广泛的开发人员工具集合使其成为寻求高效工作流程的开发人员的必备工具。 使用Crosup ，您可以告别冗长的设置过程和维护自定义 bash 脚本的需要，让您专注于您最擅长的事情——编码。

Setting up a development environment on a new Chromebook (ChromeOS) or any Linux distribution can be a time-consuming and daunting task. But fear not! Introducing Crosup, a powerful command-line tool designed to simplify and expedite the process. With Crosup, you can swiftly configure your development environment, install essential tools, and get coding in no time. In this article, we will explore the features, installation process, usage, and configuration options of Crosup, empowering you to streamline your development workflow.

Features That Make Crosup Stand Out

  1. Compatibility: Crosup is compatible with various operating systems, including ChromeOS, Debian-based Linux distributions, OpenSUSE, Fedora, RedHat, CentOS, Alpine, Arch, and Gentoo. It ensures a seamless experience regardless of your preferred platform.

  2. Easy Installation: With a single command, you can quickly install Crosup on your machine. Just run bash <(curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tsirysndr/crosup/master/install.sh) and let the magic unfold.

  3. Developer Tools Galore: Crosup provides an extensive collection of developer tools ready to be installed. From Docker, Nix, and Homebrew to VS Code, fzf, and kubectl, Crosup has you covered.

  4. Avoid Maintaining Custom Bash Scripts: Crosup eliminates the need to maintain custom bash scripts for setting up your development environment. Instead, it offers a standardized and easily configurable solution that saves you time and effort.

Installation Made Simple

To get started with Crosup, you need to execute a straightforward installation process. Just follow the command mentioned earlier, and Crosup will be up and running on your system.

Usage Made Easy

Crosup's intuitive user interface simplifies the installation of developer tools. By executing the crosup install command, you can effortlessly install various tools based on your requirements. Additionally, Crosup offers subcommands like help and init to provide further assistance and generate default configuration files.

Streamlining Your Workflow with Configuration:
Crosup employs a configuration file, Crosfile.toml, to determine the tools to be installed. By default, Crosup includes an embedded configuration, but you can generate a default configuration file using the crosup init command. The configuration file, written in HCL/TOML format, allows you to specify tools, packages, version checks, pre- and post-installation commands, and even dependencies.

Example Configuration for Debian-based System:

We'll explore a minimal Crosfile.toml configuration, which installs Vim and Git:


# Crosfile.toml
[install.pkg.vim]
packages = ["vim"]
version_check = "vim --version" [install.pkg.git]
packages = ["git"]
version_check = "git --version"

Contributing and License

Crosup is an open-source project, welcoming contributions, bug reports, and feature requests. If you're interested in improving Crosup, check out the project's CONTRIBUTING guidelines. Crosup is licensed under the MIT License, enabling you to freely use, modify, and distribute the tool.

Conclusion

Crosup is your go-to command-line tool for quickly setting up a development environment on ChromeOS and various Linux distributions. Its compatibility, simplicity, and extensive collection of developer tools make it a must-have for developers seeking an efficient workflow. With Crosup, you can bid farewell to lengthy setup processes and the need to maintain custom bash scripts, allowing you to focus on what you do best—coding.

Items.Title: BootstrapVue 入门 | Getting Started with BootstrapVue
Items.Description:
您是否希望轻松创建令人惊叹且响应迅速的在线应用程序？ 那么您来对地方了！ 使用 BootstrapVue，您可以立即快速创建美观且用户友好的界面。 这个开源工具包基于 Vue.js 和 Bootstrap 构建，非常适合开发现代 Web 应用程序。

在本文中，我们将介绍 BootstrapVue 的基础知识，并说明如何开始使用这个强大的框架。 所以，请坐下来，喝杯咖啡，让我们一起进入 BootstrapVue 的世界吧！

什么是 BootstrapVue

它被设计为高度可定制的，允许开发人员轻松修改组件的外观和行为以满足他们项目的特定需求。 它还包括对 Sass 和 Less 等 CSS 预处理器的支持，从而可以轻松自定义组件的样式。

为什么要使用 BootstrapVue？

  1. 快速开发：使用 BoostrapVue 的主要原因之一是它提供了许多可以轻松集成到您的 Web 应用程序中的预构建 UI 组件（例如按钮、表单、模式和工具提示）。
  2. 大型社区：使用 BootstrapVue 的另一个好处是它拥有一个庞大而活跃的开发人员社区，他们为框架做出贡献并为用户提供支持。
  3. 轻松迁移：BootstrapVue 使开发人员可以轻松地从 Vanilla JavaScript 切换到 Vue.js，而无需担心 Bootstrap 对 JQuery 的严重依赖。

先决条件

在我们开始安装 BootstrapVue 并了解一些组件之前，如果您有以下内容，将会有所帮助：

  • 如果您没有安装它，您可以按照安装指南进行操作。

将 BootstrapVue 与 Vue.js 集成

第一步是创建一个 Vue.js 项目。 这对于您集成 BootstrapVue 至关重要。 通过在终端中运行以下命令来创建 Vue.js 项目。

注意：BootstrapVue 目前不支持 Vue.js 3 的稳定版本。要使其正常工作，您需要将其与 vue2-3 迁移构建集成。 但是，由于此方法具有公认的局限性，因此不推荐这样做。 无论如何，您可以按照此处的步骤使用 vue2-3 迁移构建来创建新应用程序。

然后使用cd my-project导航到您的项目目录。 您现在可以继续集成 BootstrapVue。
有两种方法可以将 BootstrapVue 集成到你的 Vue.js 项目中：

  1. 使用像 NPM 和 Yarn 这样的包管理器
  2. 使用 CDN 链接

使用 NPM 或 Yarn

根据您使用的包管理器运行以下命令之一：

 # With npm npm install bootstrap bootstrap-vue # With yarn yarn add bootstrap bootstrap-vue

使用 CDN

访问CdnJS 网站获取当前 CDN 链接，然后打开 public 文件夹中的index.html文件并添加以下代码：

 <!-- Add Bootstrap and Bootstrap-Vue CSS in the <head> section --> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/bootstrap/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" /> <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/bootstrap-vue/dist/bootstrap-vue.min.css" /> <!-- Add Vue and BootstrapVue scripts just before the closing </body> tag --> <script src="https://unpkg.com/vue@2.6.14/dist/vue.min.js"></script> <script src="https://unpkg.com/bootstrap-vue@2.21.2/dist/bootstrap-vue.min.js"></script>

上面的代码会将每个包的缩小版本和最新版本拉到我们的项目中，干净简单！

对于本文，我们将坚持使用包管理器。 让我们继续设置我们已经安装的 BootstrapVue 包。

设置 BootstrapVue

设置我们刚刚安装的 Bootstrap 和 BootstrapVue 包。 在 src 文件夹中，您会找到main.js文件。 导入 BootstrapVue 及其 CSS 文件，如下所示：

 import Vue from 'vue' // Import BootstrapVue import { BootstrapVue } from 'bootstrap-vue' // Import Bootstrap and BootstrapVue CSS files ( ensure to follow the order) import 'bootstrap/dist/css/bootstrap.css' import 'bootstrap-vue/dist/bootstrap-vue.css' // Make BootstrapVue available throughout your project Vue.use(BootstrapVue)

在上面的代码中，我们使用Vue.use()函数在应用程序的入口点注册了 BootstrapVue，以便我们的应用程序可以识别它。

如果您曾经使用过 Bootstrap，就会知道它使用了一些预构建的组件。 这与 BootstrapVue 相同。 一些流行的 BootstrapVue 组件包括按钮、表单、模式、工具提示、导航菜单、旋转木马等。 这使得创建高度定制和独特的网站和应用程序成为可能。

让我们探索一些基本的 BootstrapVue 组件，包括按钮、表单和卡片。

纽扣

最基本的按钮组件是b-button ，它可以用来创建一个简单的可点击按钮。

这是一个例子：

 <template> <div class="m-5 d-flex justify-content-center"> -<b-button variant="primary">Click me!</b-button> </div> </template>;

上面的代码将创建一个带有文本“Click me!”的原色按钮。 您可以通过指定其他变体值（例如danger或success来更改按钮的颜色和样式。

您可以通过BootstrapVue 文档了解更多关于按钮组件的信息。

形式

最基本的表单组件是b-form-input ，它可以用来创建一个简单的文本输入字段。

这是一个例子：

 <template> <div class="m-5"> <b-form @submit="onSubmit" @reset="onReset" v-if="show"> <b-form-group id="input-group-1" label="Email address:" label-for="input-1" description="We'll never share your email with anyone else." > <b-form-input id="input-1" v-model="form.email" type="email" placeholder="Enter email" required ></b-form-input> </b-form-group> <b-form-group id="input-group-2" label="Your Name:" label-for="input-2"> <b-form-input id="input-2" v-model="form.name" placeholder="Enter name" required ></b-form-input> </b-form-group> <b-form-group id="input-group-3" label="Food:" label-for="input-3"> <b-form-select id="input-3" v-model="form.food" :options="foods" required ></b-form-select> </b-form-group> <b-form-group id="input-group-4" v-slot="{ ariaDescribedby }"> <b-form-checkbox-group v-model="form.checked" id="checkboxes-4" :aria-describedby="ariaDescribedby" > <b-form-checkbox value="me">Check me out</b-form-checkbox> <b-form-checkbox value="that">Check that out</b-form-checkbox> </b-form-checkbox-group> </b-form-group> <b-button type="submit" variant="primary">Submit</b-button> <b-button type="reset" variant="danger">Reset</b-button> </b-form> </div> </template> <script> export default { data() { return { form: { email: "", name: "", food: null, checked: [], }, foods: [ { text: "Select One", value: null }, "Carrots", "Beans", "Tomatoes", "Corn", ], show: true, }; }, methods: { onSubmit(event) { event.preventDefault(); alert(JSON.stringify(this.form)); }, onReset(event) { event.preventDefault(); // Reset our form values this.form.email = ""; this.form.name = ""; this.form.food = null; this.form.checked = []; // Trick to reset/clear native browser form validation state this.show = false; this.$nextTick(() => { this.show = true; }); }, }, }; </script>

上面的代码将创建一个简单的表单，请求用户的电子邮件、姓名和食物选择。 我们始终可以自定义此表单以适合我们的用例。

它通过v-validate指令、 ValidationProvider和ValidationObserver组件提供表单验证功能。

牌

最基本的卡片组件是b-card ，可用于显示图像、标题和文本。

这是一个例子：

 <template> <div class="m-5 d-flex justify-content-center"> <b-card title="Card Title" img-src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1653387137517-fbc54d488ed8?ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxzZWFyY2h8MjB8fHZ1ZXxlbnwwfHwwfHw%3D&auto=format&fit=crop&w=500&q=60" img-alt="Image" img-top tag="article" style="max-width: 20rem" class="mb-2" > <b-card-text> You can add some text in your card to suit your use case </b-card-text> <b-button href="#" variant="primary">Go somewhere</b-button> </b-card> </div> </template>

上面的代码将创建一个卡片组件，其中包含图像、标题和使用b-card组件显示的一些文本。

高级组件

其中一些高级组件是模式、轮播和工具提示。

模态

模式是在当前页面上出现的模式对话框中显示内容的一种流行方式。 这是一个例子：

 <template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-button variant="primary" @click="showModal = true">Open Modal</b-button> <b-modal v-model="showModal" title="My Modal"> <p>Hello, world!</p> </b-modal> </div> </template> <script> export default { data() { return { showModal: false, }; }, }; </script>

上面的代码将创建一个按钮，单击该按钮时，将显示一个模式对话框，标题为“My Modal”，文本为“Hello, world!”。

旋转木马

轮播是一种在旋转轮播中显示一系列图像或其他内容的流行方式。 这是一个例子：

 <template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-carousel id="carousel-1" v-model="slide" :interval="4000" controls indicators background="#ababab" img-width="1024" img-height="480" style="text-shadow: 1px 1px 2px #333" @sliding-start="onSlideStart" @sliding-end="onSlideEnd" class="w-75" > <!-- Text slides with image --> <b-carousel-slide caption="Our First slide" text="A beautiful day in the streets" img-src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=57" ></b-carousel-slide> <!-- Slides with custom text --> <b-carousel-slide img-src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=62"> <h1>Welcome to BootstrapVue</h1> </b-carousel-slide> <!-- Slides with just an image --> <b-carousel-slide text="my text" img-src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=64" ></b-carousel-slide> <!-- Slides with img slot --> <!-- Note the classes .d-block and .img-fluid to prevent browser default image alignment --> <b-carousel-slide> <template #img> <img class="d-block img-fluid w-100" width="1024" height="480" src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=50" alt="image slot" /> </template> </b-carousel-slide> </b-carousel> </div> </template>

此代码将创建一个轮播，其中包含三张包含图像和标题的幻灯片。 工具提示

当用户将鼠标悬停在某个元素上时，工具提示是一种显示附加信息的流行方式。 这是一个例子：

 <template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-button variant="primary" vb-tooltip.hover.top="'Hello, world!'" >Hover me!</b-button > </div> </template>

此代码将创建一个按钮，当鼠标悬停在该按钮上时，将显示带有文本“Hello, world!”的工具提示。

自定义 BootstrapVue 组件

自定义 BootstrapVue 组件允许您根据您的特定需求定制组件的外观和行为。 现在让我们学习如何使用示例自定义两个 BootstrapVue 组件、按钮和模式。

自定义按钮

以下是如何使用 BootstrapVue 类更改按钮的颜色和形状的示例：

 <template> <div class="m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" class="rounded-pill" >Primary Pill Button</b-button > <b-button variant="danger" class="rounded-0">Danger Square Button</b-button> </div> </template>

此代码将创建两个按钮，一个具有原色和圆形药丸形状，另一个具有危险色和方形。 您还可以使用类或样式属性向按钮添加自定义class或style ：

 <template> <div class="m-5 d-flex justify-content-center"> <b-button variant="success" class="my-custom-class" style="font-size: 1.2rem" >Customized Button</b-button > </div> </template>

此代码将创建一个成功按钮，其中包含自定义类my-custom-class和自定义样式font-size: 1.2rem; 自定义模态

以下是如何使用 BootstrapVue 道具更改模态框的大小和位置的示例：

 <template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-button variant="primary" @click="showModal = true">Open Modal</b-button> <b-modal v-model="showModal" title="My Modal" size="lg" centered> <p>Hello, world!</p> </b-modal> </div> </template> <script> export default { data() { return { showModal: false, }; }, }; </script>

此代码将创建一个按钮，单击该按钮时，将显示一个模式对话框，标题为“My Modal”，文本为“Hello, world!” 具有大尺寸和居中位置。

您还可以使用 slots 或style属性向模式添加自定义内容或样式：

 <template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" @click="showModal = true">Open Modal</b-button> <b-modal v-model="showModal" title="My Modal"> <template #default> <h2>Custom Heading</h2> <p>This is some custom content.</p> </template> <template #modal-footer="{ ok }"> <b-button @click="ok()">Custom OK Button</b-button> </template> <template #modal-body="{ css }"> <p :style="css">This paragraph has custom CSS.</p> </template> </b-modal> </div> </template> <script> export default { data() { return { showModal: false, }; }, }; </script>

此代码将创建一个带有自定义标题和内容、自定义确定按钮和带有自定义 CSS 的段落的模态对话框。

使用 BootstrapVue 设计样式

在本文中，我们将解释如何使用 CSS 为 BootstrapVue 组件设置样式。

样式组件

您可以通过向组件添加class或style属性来将样式应用于组件：

 <template> <div class="m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" class="my-custom-class" style="background-color: red" >Custom Button</b-button > </div> </template>

在上面的代码中，我们为按钮添加了自定义类my-custom-class和自定义样式background-color: red 。 这会将按钮的背景颜色更改为红色。

使用预处理器允许您编写更强大和模块化的 CSS 代码。 要在 BootstrapVue 中使用预处理器，您只需将预处理器文件包含在您的项目中并将其导入您的代码中：

 /* In your main.scss file */ @import "~bootstrap/scss/bootstrap"; @import "~bootstrap-vue/src/index.scss";

导入预处理器文件后，您可以使用它们的功能来设计组件的样式。 例如，在 Sass 中，您可以使用变量来定义颜色和其他属性：

 $primary-color: #007bff; .custom-button { background-color: $primary-color; border-radius: 0.25rem; }

在这个例子中，我们为原色定义了一个变量，我们用它来为自定义按钮设置自定义类的样式custom-button 。 这将为按钮提供#007bff （原色）的背景颜色和0.25rem的边框半径。

作用域样式

有时您可能希望将样式应用于仅影响该组件而不影响页面上任何其他组件的组件。 在这种情况下，您可以使用范围样式。 作用域样式仅适用于特定组件及其子组件。

要在 BootstrapVue 中使用范围样式，您可以将scoped属性添加到组件中的<style>标记：

 <template> <div class="m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" class="my-custom-class m-2" >Custom Button</b-button > <b-button variant="primary">Custom Button</b-button> <b-button variant="primary">Custom Button</b-button> </div> </template> <style scoped> .my-custom-class { background-color: red; } </style>

在这个例子中，我们向<style>标签添加了一个scoped属性，并为my-custom-class类定义了一个样式。 此样式将仅应用于此组件中的按钮，而不应用于页面上的任何其他按钮。

结论

在本文中，我们介绍了 BootstrapVue 的基础知识，包括其安装和设置、配置和使用。 我们已经探索了它的一些关键组件，例如按钮、模式、工具提示等。

无论您是构建简单的网站还是复杂的 Web 应用程序，它都可以帮助您入门并使构建用户界面变得更加容易。



原文:

Are you looking to create stunning and responsive online applications easily? Then you've come to the right place! With BootstrapVue, you can quickly create beautiful and user-friendly interfaces in no time. This open-source toolkit is built on Vue.js and Bootstrap, which is perfect for developing modern web applications.

In this article, we'll go through the fundamentals of BootstrapVue and illustrate how to get started with this powerful framework. So, please sit back, grab a cup of coffee, and let's dive into the world of BootstrapVue!

What is BootstrapVue

BootstrapVue is a popular open-source front-end framework that combines Bootstrap (a front-end UI framework) and vue.js (a progressive JavaScript framework) to create reusable UI components and web applications.

It is designed to be highly customizable, allowing developers to easily modify the appearance and behavior of the components to fit the specific needs of their project. It also includes support for CSS preprocessors like Sass and Less, making it easy to customize the styles of the components.

Why use BootstrapVue?

  1. Fast Development: One of the main reasons to use BoostrapVue is that it provides many pre-built UI components (such as buttons, forms, modals, and tooltips) that can be easily integrated into your web application.
  2. Large Community: Another benefit of using BootstrapVue is that it has a large and active community of developers who contribute to the framework and provide support to users.
  3. Easy Migration: BootstrapVue makes it easy for developers to switch from Vanilla JavaScript to Vue.js without bothering about Bootstrap's heavy dependency on JQuery.

Prerequisites

Before we start installing BootstrapVue and learning about some components, it will help if you have the following:

  • Basic knowledge of Bootstrap.
  • Basic knowledge of Vue.js.
  • Vue CLI tool globally installed on your machine. You can follow the installation guide if you don't have it installed.

Integrating BootstrapVue with Vue.js

The first step would be to create a Vue.js project. This is essential for you to integrate BootstrapVue. Create a Vue.js project by running the following commands in your terminal.

Note: BootstrapVue does not presently support the stable version of Vue.js 3. To get it working, you'd need to integrate it with the vue2-3 migration build. However, this is not recommended due to recognized limitations with this method. Regardless, you can use the vue2-3 migration build to create a new application by following the steps here.

Then navigate into your project’s directory using cd my-project. You can now proceed to integrate BootstrapVue.
There are two ways to integrate BootstrapVue into your Vue.js project:

  1. Using a package manager like NPM and Yarn
  2. Using CDN links

Using NPM or Yarn

Run one of the commands below depending on the package manager you are working with:

# With npm
npm install bootstrap bootstrap-vue # With yarn
yarn add bootstrap bootstrap-vue

Using CDN

Visit CdnJS website to get the current CDN link, then open the index.html file in the public folder and add the code below:

<!-- Add Bootstrap and Bootstrap-Vue CSS in the <head> section -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/bootstrap/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css"
/>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/bootstrap-vue/dist/bootstrap-vue.min.css"
/> <!-- Add Vue and BootstrapVue scripts just before the closing </body> tag -->
<script src="https://unpkg.com/vue@2.6.14/dist/vue.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://unpkg.com/bootstrap-vue@2.21.2/dist/bootstrap-vue.min.js"></script>

The code above will pull each package's minified and the latest version into our project, clean and simple!

For this article, we will stick to using package managers. Let’s continue setting up the BootstrapVue package we have installed.

Setting up BootstrapVue

To set up the Bootstrap and BootstrapVue packages we just installed. In the src folder, you will find the main.js file. Import BootstrapVue, and its CSS files as seen below:

import Vue from 'vue' // Import BootstrapVue
import { BootstrapVue } from 'bootstrap-vue' // Import Bootstrap and BootstrapVue CSS files ( ensure to follow the order)
import 'bootstrap/dist/css/bootstrap.css'
import 'bootstrap-vue/dist/bootstrap-vue.css' // Make BootstrapVue available throughout your project
Vue.use(BootstrapVue)

In the code above, we registered BootstrapVue in the application's entry point using the Vue.use() function so our application can recognize it.

BootstrapVue Components

If you have ever used Bootstrap, you will know it uses some pre-built components. This is the same with BootstrapVue. BootstrapVue components are built specifically for Vue.js, making them easier to use and integrate into your Vue.js applications.

Some popular BootstrapVue components include buttons, forms, modals, tooltips, navigation menus, carousels, and more. BootstrapVue also includes some unique components not available in standard Bootstrap, such as the BTable component for creating dynamic and interactive tables.

BootstrapVue also includes a range of utility classes and mixins that can customize the appearance and behavior of components even further. This makes it possible to create highly customized and unique websites and applications that stand out.

Let’s explore some basic BootstrapVue components, including buttons, forms, and cards.

Buttons

BootstrapVue provides a variety of button components that can be used to create different types of buttons with various styles and functionalities. The most basic button component is the b-button, which can be used to create a simple clickable button.

Here is an example:

<template> <div class="m-5 d-flex justify-content-center"> -<b-button variant="primary">Click me!</b-button> </div>
</template>;

This code above will create a primary-colored button with the text "Click me!" — this is because the variant prop is set to primary. You can change the color and style of the button by specifying other variant values, such as danger or success.

BootstrapVue also provides other button components that can be used to create dropdown menus, toggle buttons, and more. You can learn more about button components via the BootstrapVue documentation.

Forms

BootstrapVue provides a variety of form components that can be used to create forms with different types of input fields, labels, and validations. The most basic form component is b-form-input, which can be used to create a simple text input field.

Here is an example:

<template> <div class="m-5"> <b-form @submit="onSubmit" @reset="onReset" v-if="show"> <b-form-group id="input-group-1" label="Email address:" label-for="input-1" description="We'll never share your email with anyone else." > <b-form-input id="input-1" v-model="form.email" type="email" placeholder="Enter email" required ></b-form-input> </b-form-group> <b-form-group id="input-group-2" label="Your Name:" label-for="input-2"> <b-form-input id="input-2" v-model="form.name" placeholder="Enter name" required ></b-form-input> </b-form-group> <b-form-group id="input-group-3" label="Food:" label-for="input-3"> <b-form-select id="input-3" v-model="form.food" :options="foods" required ></b-form-select> </b-form-group> <b-form-group id="input-group-4" v-slot="{ ariaDescribedby }"> <b-form-checkbox-group v-model="form.checked" id="checkboxes-4" :aria-describedby="ariaDescribedby" > <b-form-checkbox value="me">Check me out</b-form-checkbox> <b-form-checkbox value="that">Check that out</b-form-checkbox> </b-form-checkbox-group> </b-form-group> <b-button type="submit" variant="primary">Submit</b-button> <b-button type="reset" variant="danger">Reset</b-button> </b-form> </div>
</template> <script>
export default { data() { return { form: { email: "", name: "", food: null, checked: [], }, foods: [ { text: "Select One", value: null }, "Carrots", "Beans", "Tomatoes", "Corn", ], show: true, }; }, methods: { onSubmit(event) { event.preventDefault(); alert(JSON.stringify(this.form)); }, onReset(event) { event.preventDefault(); // Reset our form values this.form.email = ""; this.form.name = ""; this.form.food = null; this.form.checked = []; // Trick to reset/clear native browser form validation state this.show = false; this.$nextTick(() => { this.show = true; }); }, },
};
</script>

The code above will create a simple form requesting the user's email, name, and food choice. We can always customize this form to suit our use-case.

BootstrapVue provides other form components that can be used to create radio buttons, checkboxes, dropdown menus, and more. It provides form validation functionality through the v-validate directive, the ValidationProvider, and ValidationObserver components.

Cards

BootstrapVue provides a variety of card components that can be used to style text, headings, and images. The most basic card component is the b-card, which can be used to display an image, a heading, and text.

Here is an example:

<template> <div class="m-5 d-flex justify-content-center"> <b-card title="Card Title" img-src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1653387137517-fbc54d488ed8?ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxzZWFyY2h8MjB8fHZ1ZXxlbnwwfHwwfHw%3D&auto=format&fit=crop&w=500&q=60" img-alt="Image" img-top tag="article" style="max-width: 20rem" class="mb-2" > <b-card-text> You can add some text in your card to suit your use case </b-card-text> <b-button href="#" variant="primary">Go somewhere</b-button> </b-card> </div>
</template>

The code above will create a card component with an image, a title, and some text displayed using the b-card component.

BootstrapVue provides other card components that can be used to create headings, paragraphs, lists, and more. BootstrapVue also provides a utility class system for card-related styles, in which you can apply common styles like text color, font weight, and text alignment.

Advanced Components

BootstrapVue provides many advanced components that can be used to add more complex functionality to your Vue.js applications. Some of these advanced components are modals, carousels, and tooltips.

Modals

Modals are a popular way to display content in a modal dialog that appears on the current page. BootstrapVue provides a b-modal component that can be used to create modals with various functionalities, such as showing/hiding the modal, changing its size, and adding custom content.

Here is an example:

<template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-button variant="primary" @click="showModal = true">Open Modal</b-button> <b-modal v-model="showModal" title="My Modal"> <p>Hello, world!</p> </b-modal> </div>
</template>
<script>
export default { data() { return { showModal: false, }; },
};
</script>

The code above will create a button that, when clicked, will show a modal dialog with the title "My Modal" and the text "Hello, world!".

BootstrapVue also provides other modal-related components that can be used to create confirmation dialogs, scrollable modals, and more. BootstrapVue also provides events that can be used to trigger actions before and after the modal is shown or hidden.

Carousels

Carousels are a popular way to display a series of images or other content in a rotating carousel. BootstrapVue provides a b-carousel component that can be used to create carousels with various functionalities, such as autoplay, navigation controls, and indicators.

Here is an example:

<template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-carousel id="carousel-1" v-model="slide" :interval="4000" controls indicators background="#ababab" img-width="1024" img-height="480" style="text-shadow: 1px 1px 2px #333" @sliding-start="onSlideStart" @sliding-end="onSlideEnd" class="w-75" > <!-- Text slides with image --> <b-carousel-slide caption="Our First slide" text="A beautiful day in the streets" img-src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=57" ></b-carousel-slide> <!-- Slides with custom text --> <b-carousel-slide img-src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=62"> <h1>Welcome to BootstrapVue</h1> </b-carousel-slide> <!-- Slides with just an image --> <b-carousel-slide text="my text" img-src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=64" ></b-carousel-slide> <!-- Slides with img slot --> <!-- Note the classes .d-block and .img-fluid to prevent browser default image alignment --> <b-carousel-slide> <template #img> <img class="d-block img-fluid w-100" width="1024" height="480" src="https://picsum.photos/1024/480/?image=50" alt="image slot" /> </template> </b-carousel-slide> </b-carousel> </div>
</template>

This code will create a carousel with three slides containing an image and a caption. BootstrapVue also provides other carousel-related components that can be used to create slide transitions, fade transitions, and provides events that can be used to trigger actions before and after each slide is shown.

Tooltips

Tooltips are a popular way to display additional information when the user hovers over an element. BootstrapVue provides a b-tooltip component that can be used to create tooltips with various functionalities, such as changing the position, adding custom content, and controlling when the tooltip is shown.

Here is an example:

<template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-button variant="primary" v-b-tooltip.hover.top="'Hello, world!'" >Hover me!</b-button > </div>
</template>

This code will create a button that, when hovered over, will show a tooltip with the text "Hello, world!".

BootstrapVue also provides other tooltip-related components that can be used to create tooltips with HTML content, programmatically show/hide tooltips, and more.

Customizing BootstrapVue components

Customizing BootstrapVue components allows you to tailor the appearance and behavior of the components to your specific needs. Let’s now learn how to customize two BootstrapVue components, buttons, and modals using examples.

Customizing Buttons

BootstrapVue provides many customization options for buttons, such as changing the size, color, shape and adding custom classes or styles.

Here is an example of how to change the color and shape of a button using BootstrapVue classes:

<template> <div class="m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" class="rounded-pill" >Primary Pill Button</b-button > <b-button variant="danger" class="rounded-0">Danger Square Button</b-button> </div>
</template>

This code will create two buttons, one with a primary color and a rounded pill shape, and one with a danger color and a square shape. The rounded-pill and rounded-0 classes are BootstrapVue classes that control the border radius of the button.

You can also add custom classes or styles to the button by using the class or style attributes:

<template> <div class="m-5 d-flex justify-content-center"> <b-button variant="success" class="my-custom-class" style="font-size: 1.2rem" >Customized Button</b-button > </div>
</template>

This code will create a success button with a custom class of my-custom-class and a custom style of font-size: 1.2rem;.

Customizing Modals

BootstrapVue provides many customization options for modals, such as changing the size, position, backdrop and adding custom content or styles.

Here is an example of how to change the size and position of a modal using BootstrapVue props:

<template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center align-items-center" style="height: 100vh" > <b-button variant="primary" @click="showModal = true">Open Modal</b-button> <b-modal v-model="showModal" title="My Modal" size="lg" centered> <p>Hello, world!</p> </b-modal> </div>
</template>
<script>
export default { data() { return { showModal: false, }; },
};
</script>

This code will create a button that, when clicked, will show a modal dialog with the title "My Modal" and the text "Hello, world!" with a large size and centered position.

You can also add custom content or styles to the modal by using slots or the style attribute:

<template> <div class="d-flex justify-content-center m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" @click="showModal = true">Open Modal</b-button> <b-modal v-model="showModal" title="My Modal"> <template #default> <h2>Custom Heading</h2> <p>This is some custom content.</p> </template> <template #modal-footer="{ ok }"> <b-button @click="ok()">Custom OK Button</b-button> </template> <template #modal-body="{ css }"> <p :style="css">This paragraph has custom CSS.</p> </template> </b-modal> </div>
</template>
<script>
export default { data() { return { showModal: false, }; },
};
</script>

This code will create a modal dialog with a custom heading and content, a custom OK button, and a paragraph with custom CSS.

Styling with BootstrapVue

BootstrapVue provides powerful styling features that allow you to style your components with CSS easily. In this article, we will provide an explanation of how to style BootstrapVue components using CSS.

Styling Components

BootstrapVue components have a set of default styles that you can easily customize. You can apply styles to a component by adding a class or style attribute to the component:

<template> <div class="m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" class="my-custom-class" style="background-color: red" >Custom Button</b-button > </div>
</template>

In the code above, we've added a custom class of my-custom-class to the button and a custom style of background-color: red. This will change the background color of the button to red.

CSS Preprocessors

BootstrapVue also supports the use of CSS preprocessors like Sass and Less. Using a preprocessor allows you to write more powerful and modular CSS code. To use a preprocessor with BootstrapVue, you can simply include the preprocessor file in your project and import it into your code:

/* In your main.scss file */
@import "~bootstrap/scss/bootstrap";
@import "~bootstrap-vue/src/index.scss";

Once you've imported the preprocessor files, you can use their features to style your components. For example, in Sass, you can use variables to define colors and other properties:

$primary-color: #007bff; .custom-button { background-color: $primary-color; border-radius: 0.25rem;
}

In this example, we've defined a variable for the primary color, and we've used it to style a custom button with a custom class of custom-button. This will give the button a background color of #007bff (the primary color) and a border radius of 0.25rem.

Scoped Styles

Sometimes you may want to apply styles to a component that only affects that component and not any other components on the page. In this case, you can use scoped styles. Scoped styles are only applied to a specific component and its children.

To use scoped styles in BootstrapVue, you can add a scoped attribute to the <style> tag in your component:

<template> <div class="m-5"> <b-button variant="primary" class="my-custom-class m-2" >Custom Button</b-button > <b-button variant="primary">Custom Button</b-button> <b-button variant="primary">Custom Button</b-button> </div>
</template>
<style scoped>
.my-custom-class { background-color: red;
}
</style>

In this example, we've added a scoped attribute to the <style> tag, and we've defined a style for the my-custom-class class. This style will only be applied to the button in this component and not to any other buttons on the page.

Conclusion

In this article, we have covered the basics of BootstrapVue, including its installation and setup, configuration, and usage. We have explored some of its key components, such as buttons, modals, tooltips, and others.

BootstrapVue is a powerful tool that can help developers create beautiful, responsive web applications quickly and easily. Whether you are building a simple website or a complex web application, it can help you get started and make building user interfaces much easier.

Items.Title: 使用 Power Virtual Agents 创建多语言聊天机器人：入门的 3 种解决方法 | Creating Multilingual Chatbots with Power Virtual Agents: 3 Workarounds to Get Started
Items.Description:
照片由Joshua Hoehne在Unsplash上拍摄

动机

可以通过三种不同的方式回答我们“一天中的 Power Virtual Agents”中两位客人最常见的问题。 在这里，我将讨论这些问题的答案：

  1. 我如何使用平台 23 种可用语言不支持的语言使用 Power Virtual Agents？

  2. 我能否在 Power Virtual Agents 上开发一个可以响应不同语言的聊天机器人，而不是为每种语言创建单独的机器人？

如今，Power Virtual Agents 支持23 种语言，包括预览版语言，每个聊天机器人指定一种语言。 要完成更多工作，需要一种变通方法。

三种解决方法

使用 Power Automate 流程

使用 Power Automate 将 Power Virtual Agent 扩展为多语言 QnA Resolver。 该解决方案涉及 Power Automate、Azure 的自定义问答和 Microsoft Translator v2 服务。 它解决了这两个问题； 但是，请记住，机器人可以用任何语言响应查询，但其用户界面将保留您的主要机器人的语言。

几个月前我写了一篇文章，提供了一个循序渐进的指南。 你可以使用它，但需要 Azure 订阅来托管附加服务。

使用 Dynamics 365 Customer Service 并为支持语音的 PVA 创建多语言 IVR

使用各种工作流和级联队列，创建一个能够将不同语言的对话引导到不同机器人的礼宾机器人。 这种方法得益于与电话语音的兼容性。

礼宾服务背后的基本概念是建立一系列驻留在不同队列中的机器人。 永远记住包括一个升级路径到人工代理。

从语言选择器开始，并将结果转发到为该特定语言指定的队列。 您可以为该队列创建另一个机器人或分配人工代理。 此方法可用于具有多种语言的多种技能的级联机器人，作为支持您的多语言用户和我们的人工代理的机器人工厂的基础。 例如，请找到我的分步指南，使用 3 种语言的选择器配置它。 缺点是这仅适用于 D365 客户服务。

中继上的翻译机器人

对于这种方法，您将需要一个 Azure 服务机器人和 Azure 认知服务转换器来与您的 Power Virtual Agents 一起工作。 这可能看起来令人生畏，但这是最终的解决方案，直到 Microsoft 发布 Power Virtual Agents 的多语言版本。

基本思想涉及通过 DirectLine API 连接您的 Power Virtual Agents Bot 并使用各种语言的所有主题。 这是使用中间件 (Azure Bot) 在用户和 Power Virtual Agents Bot 之间转换消息来完成的。 中间件将使用认知服务在整个对话过程中翻译文本。

您甚至会找到 ARM 模板来部署您需要的资源。



原文:

Photo by Joshua Hoehne on Unsplash

Motivation

Two of our "Power Virtual Agents in a Day" guests' most frequently asked questions can be answered in three distinct ways. Herein, I will discuss the answers to these queries:

  1. How can I utilize Power Virtual Agents in a language not supported by the platform's 23 available languages?

  2. Can I develop a single chatbot on Power Virtual Agents that can respond to different languages instead of creating separate bots for each language?

Today, Power Virtual Agents support 23 languages, including those in preview, with one language designated for each chatbot. To accomplish more, a workaround is necessary.

Three workarounds

Using a Power Automate flow

Use Power Automate to expand Power Virtual Agent into a multilingual QnA Resolver. This solution involves Power Automate, Azure's Custom Question Answering, and Microsoft Translator v2 service. It addresses both questions; however, remember that the bot can respond to queries in any language, but its user interface will remain in your main bot's language.

I authored an article a few months ago that provides a step-by-step guide. You can use it but need an Azure subscription to host the additional services.

Use Dynamics 365 Customer Service and create a multilingual IVR for voice-enabled PVA

Using various workstreams and cascaded queues, create a concierge bot capable of directing the dialogue in different languages to different bots. This approach benefits from compatibility with voice via telephony.

The fundamental concept behind the concierge is establishing a cascade of bots residing in separate queues. Always remember to include an escalation path to a human agent.

Begin with a language selector and forward the result to a queue designated for that specific language. You can create another bot for that queue or assign human agents. This method can be employed for a cascade of bots with diverse skills in multiple languages, serving as the foundation for a bot factory that supports your multilingual users and our human agents. As an example, please find my step-by-step guide for configuring this with a 3-language selector. The downside is that this is only available with D365 Customer Service.

Translation Bot on a relay

For this approach, you will need an Azure Service Bot and Azure Cognitive Services Translator to work with your Power Virtual Agents. It may seem daunting, but this is the ultimate solution until Microsoft releases a multilingual version of Power Virtual Agents.

The basic idea involves connecting your Power Virtual Agents Bot through the DirectLine API and utilizing all its topics in various languages. This is done using a middleware (Azure Bot) to translate messages between the user and the Power Virtual Agents Bot. The middleware will use Cognitive Services to translate the text throughout the conversation.

Microsoft created an excellent step-by-step guide and a repository on GitHub with all elements to set this up. You will even find ARM templates to deploy the resources you need.

Items.Title: 使用 GraphQL 和流式数据库查询实时数据 | Query Real-time Data With GraphQL And Streaming Database
Items.Description:
在现代应用程序开发中，高效查询和检索实时数据对于构建健壮和高性能的系统至关重要。 使用物化视图，我们可以提高查询性能。 当它与 GraphQL 和流式数据库结合使用时，开发人员可以定义他们的查询以利用这些不断变化的数据的物化视图，从而使他们能够构建高度响应和交互的应用程序。

例如，Twitter 等社交媒体平台每秒都会产生大量数据。 此数据对于分析趋势和用户行为很有价值。 在本文中，我们将探讨如何集成 GraphQL、物化视图和流式数据库（例如RisingWave） ，使我们能够高效地查询推文并实时发现最热门的主题标签。

在深入实施之前，了解这 3 个概念（GraphQL、物化视图和流式数据库）非常重要。 我相信你足够聪明，可以使用Google或ChatGPT来查找这些信息。 但是，我将在下一节中很快解释为什么这种集成是有益的，以及每种集成的作用。

作为预计算缓存的物化视图

使用物化视图，我们可以预先计算并存储频繁执行的 SQL 查询的结果。 它是数据的非规范化表示，这意味着复杂的连接和聚合已经执行并存储在像PostgreSQL这样的数据库中。 这简化了检索数据所需的查询逻辑，并消除了我们手动处理连接操作的需要。 在 Twitter 的案例中，物化视图可用于计算用户活动的摘要，例如关注者的数量、使用的主题标签、喜欢或评论。

与 REST 不同，GraphQL 显着减少了往返次数，从而加快了数据获取速度。 使用 GraphQL，我们可以通过定义的模式直接访问数据库中的物化视图，抽象掉底层数据库结构的复杂性。 社交媒体分析平台可以利用 GraphQL 提供灵活的 API 来查询和分析用户生成的内容。

有几种流行的 GraphQL 构建器和框架可以帮助构建 GraphQL API。 您可以连接到不同的数据源并与流行的数据库（如 PostgreSQL 和 MySQL）集成。 以下是一些广泛使用的：

  • 阿波罗服务器
  • 叶修
  • 关系
  • 立方体
  • 步曾

在演示部分，我将展示如何使用 StepZen 在声明性代码中构建 GraphQL API。

流式数据库处理实时数据

为了充分利用 GraphQL实时数据查询的全部潜力，可以使用流式数据库。 物化视图可能并不总是包含最新的数据，因为它们取决于视图刷新的时间和频率。 要获得更新的结果，您需要重复执行查询。 而像RisingWave这样的流式数据库几乎在写入时完成所有工作。 这篇 文章解释了流式数据库与传统数据库的区别。 当数据流入流式数据库时，它会被处理并立即用于更新现有的物化视图。 它可以从Kafka或Pulsar等各种数据源获取数据。 通过将 GraphQL 与流式数据库相结合，我们可以持续摄取传入的推文帖子并实时更新物化视图，并即时查询数据变化。

您可以从 RisingWave 查询Source 、 Table和Materialized View 。

查询 Twitter 事件演示

一旦我们理解了 GraphQL、物化视图和流式数据库的重要性，我们就可以使用这种组合来创建一种访问推文数据的新方法。 通过预先计算数据摘要并通过 GraphQL 端点公开它们，我们可以快速从推文中获得有价值的见解。

在你开始之前

在本教程中，我们将利用名为快速 Twitter 事件处理的网站上现有的 RisingWave 用例演示。 确保您通过克隆、使用Docker在此处启动演示项目、将 RisingWave 连接到 Kafka 数据流以及按照教程指导您定义实体化视图来完成本教程。

安装的其他先决条件是：

  • 安装了 ngrok (Mac/Windows)

当你准备好的时候

现在我假设您配置了 RisingWave，并且我们有一个名为hot_hashtags的物化视图，该视图由 RisingWave 处理，用于跟踪每个主题标签每天在 Twitter 上的使用频率。 在接下来的步骤中，我们安装并设置StepZen，设计 GraphQL 模式，将 GraphQL 查询映射到物化视图中的实际数据，最后公开 GraphQL 端点。

第 1 步：安装 StepZen CLI

请注意，您也可以按照 StepZen 网站上的说明进行安装和运行。 运行以下命令来安装 StepZen CLI：

第 2 步：在 Docker 中运行 StepZen

接下来，我们使用在上一步中安装的 StepZen CLI 在您的本地计算机上运行 StepZen 服务：

要使用 StepZen CLI 进行本地开发，您必须通过将 CLI 指向本地配置来登录。 只需在 StepZen 服务启动后运行以下命令：

 stepzen login --config ~/.stepzen/stepzen-config.local.yaml

第 3 步：克隆graphql-stepzen-risingwave演示项目

在 GitHub 上找到我名为graphql-stepzen-risingwave-demo的存储库。 这个项目已经有了你需要的一切。 在postgresql.graphql GraphQL 模式定义语言 (SDL) 文件中有书面模式代码，其中为 RisingWave 数据库中的物化视图hot_hashtags定义了类型和查询。 它还有一个包含我们的 GraphQL 端点的stepzen.config.json文件。

 git clone https://github.com/Boburmirzo/graphql-stepzen-risingwave-demo.git cd graphql-stepzen-risingwave-demo

第 4 步：创建 ngrok 帐户并安装 ngrok（可选）

请注意，如果您在本地环境中运行 StepZen 和 RisingWave，这只是一个额外的步骤。 对于可通过互联网访问的云或服务器中正在运行的实例，您可以在 StepZen 数据库配置中设置 RisingWave 的直接地址。 如果您在 Docker 容器中同时运行 StepZen 和 RisingWave，您可以指定host.docker.internal的地址。

为 ngrok 创建一个帐户，并确保您可以通过设置authtoken来访问它。 要使 Risingwave 数据库可用于您的专用网络之外的其他服务，您需要创建一个 TCP 隧道。 为此，ngrok 将用于运行 RisingWave 的端口4566 ：

运行该命令后，Ngrok 将返回本地 RisingWave 数据库的转发地址，如下所示： 0.tcp.eu.ngrok.io:14304

您需要将其添加到演示项目中的文件./config.yaml中，您需要将{{ngrok_tunnel}}替换为您的地址0.tcp.ngrok.io:15650 ：

 configurationset: - configuration: name: postgresql_config uri: postgresql://root:@0.tcp.eu.ngrok.io:15650/dev

第 5 步：运行 GraphQL 端点

通过运行命令stepzen start ，您可以将我们在postgresql.graphql文件中的 GraphQL 模式部署到 StepZen。 这会立即在本地主机中创建一个 GraphQL API，可通过stepzen.config.json文件中配置的端点 ( api/twitter ) 访问。 如果您导航到localhost:5001/api/twitter ，您将在浏览器上看到 StepZen 仪表板资源管理器。

第六步：查询物化视图

当您运行以下查询getHotHashtags时，它将从 RisingWave 实体化视图中提取数据并在资源管理器中显示返回的数据。

 query MyQuery { getHotHashtags { hashtag hashtag_occurrences window_start } }

查看输出：

如果你打开postgresql.graphql文件，你会看到我是如何使用 GraphQL 指令@dbquery连接数据库并编写 SQL 查询来选择 TOP 10 流行主题标签的。

 type Query { getHotHashtags: [hot_hashtags] @dbquery( type: "postgresql" query: """ SELECT * FROM hot_hashtags ORDER BY hashtag_occurrences DESC LIMIT 10 """ configuration: "postgresql_config" ) }

下一步

到目前为止，我们已经构建并部署了一个带有数据库后端的 GraphQL API api/twitter 。 接下来，您可以观察如何使用 RisingWave 使用来自 Kafka 以外的其他来源的实时数据，组合多个数据流，在连接的流上创建物化视图，以及使用 StepZen 创建一系列查询。

结论

借助 StepZen GraphQL API，我们简化了数据访问，而无需引入后端服务来完成这项工作，并将其与 RisingWave 提供的实时更新相结合。 总之，使用 GraphQL 和流式数据库查询实时数据为创建高度响应和交互的应用程序开辟了新的可能性。
